action.reasoning: Observation: Huber robust LightGBM is the new best with ic_mean=0.058284 and substantially higher rank_ic, suggesting outlier-robust objectives generalize better than squared loss. Analysis: train_obj.py already supports regression_l1 and fair; L1 is the most direct robust alternative and should be comparable in runtime to Huber. Decision: train a 10-model LightGBM regression_l1 ensemble on 2008-2021 using the same presets, smoke-test predict.py locally, and submit the checkpoint for evaluation.
action.command: python train_obj.py --out checkpoints/l1_v1 --objective regression_l1 --num_models 10 --include_valid_train --num_threads 20 && python predict.py --data_path /data --checkpoint checkpoints/l1_v1 --output local_pred_l1_v1.json
